guest: Ben Askren, Olympic wrestler and former UFC/Bellator/ONE champion turned investor and entrepreneur

business_ideas.idea: A course/teaching product on handling failure and competition psychology, drawn from his fight career
Rokfin: token-incentivized creator subscription platform he's involved with
Expanding his chain of wrestling academies

predictions.text: Independent creator platforms will keep pulling talent and audience away from legacy media gatekeepers

disagreements: Ben is bullish on crypto-token creator economics; the hosts probe whether tiny platforms like Rokfin can ever bootstrap network effects, and whether most fighters can realistically transition to business.
dynamics: The hosts mostly interview and let Ben talk; Shaan digs into the psychology of the 5-second knockout while Sam pushes on the business ventures. Ben is blunt, unbothered, and analytical about his own failures.
frameworks: Three responses to failure: when you meet failure you dig in, change approach, or move on
Competing as a separate skill: performing under pressure is trainable, distinct from the core skill
Deliberate exposure: seek out high-pressure moments to build tolerance
learnings: Processing a public humiliation analytically instead of emotionally is what lets elite competitors continue
Fighter economics are brutal at the bottom — fame vastly exceeds pay for most of the roster
An athlete's distribution (audience and credibility) is the real asset to build businesses on

banger_quotes.quote: When you meet failure, there's really three things that you can do
You need to put yourself in as many as high pressure moments

numbers_dropped: Ben's MMA record: 19 wins, 2 losses across 21 fights
Rokfin cited at only ~3,500 subscribers at recording
Low-end UFC pay: ~$10K to show plus ~$10K to win per fight
notable: Ben dissects being knocked out in 5 seconds by Jorge Masvidal — the fastest KO in UFC history — with total detachment.
